comment: tai_cdc — gray-code 2-FF synchronizer (Cummings) bringing the TAI value from the taisoc/tai domain into the MAC domain, metastability-safe. The NIC stamps with TAI_MAC (MAC-domain), never raw cross-domain TAI. No discipline anywhere in the clock set: taiosc is the reference by definition.

config_nodes.name: TAI_CDC_POWER
config_nodes.comment: power/enable bit: sync runs on the MAC edge while bit0 = 1 (set once)

config_nodes.name: TAI_CDC_RUN_UNTIL
config_nodes.comment: configured self-run tick budget (set once by starter)

config_nodes.name: TAI_IN
config_nodes.comment: sampled tai value presented on tai_cdc's input lane (source-domain sample; module-barrier: not a raw read of tai's registers)

dff_nodes.name: TAI_CDC_TICKS
dff_nodes.comment: self-run tick counter (run-loop bookkeeping; bounds the self-run)

dff_nodes.name: TAI_SYNC1_GRAY
dff_nodes.comment: stage-1 sync FF (gray-coded; catches the metastability window)

comb_nodes.name: TAI_MAC
comb_nodes.comment: gray-decoded stage-2 output: the MAC-domain TAI value (NIC reads this)
